name: Snapper
description: Known for its vibrant color, snapper is a popular fish caught in Zamboanga’s waters.
location: Zamboanga del Norte, Zamboanga del Sur

name: Spanner Crab
description: A rare, deep-sea crab species that’s highly prized in local cuisine, especially in Zamboanga City.
location: Zamboanga City

name: Anchovy
description: Small but economically important, dilis is often dried and used as a condiment or side dish.
location: Zamboanga coastline

name: Batfish
description: Characterized by its disc-like shape and long fins, this fish is common in Zamboanga’s coral reefs.
location: Zamboanga Peninsula

name: Goby
description: Small, often colorful fish found in the rocky tidal zones and reefs of Zamboanga’s coastal areas.
location: Zamboanga del Norte, Zamboanga

name: Tabios
description: The world’s smallest commercially harvested fish, endemic to the Philippines.
location: Zamboanga Peninsula

name: Giant Tiger Prawn
description: A key aquaculture species grown in the mangrove areas of Zamboanga.
location: Zamboanga City, Zamboanga del Norte

name: Sardine
description: Small oily fish found in large schools, widely used in the production of canned sardines, especially in Zamboanga City.
location: Zamboanga City

name: Spanish Mackerel
description: A fast-swimming predatory fish that is economically significant in Zamboanga’s fishing industry.
location: Zamboanga del Norte, Sulu Sea

name: Zamboanga Goby
description: A native goby species inhabiting estuaries, brackish mangroves, and river mouths. Plays an important role in coastal food webs.
location: Estuaries and river mouths of Zamboanga del Norte and Zamboanga del Sur.

name: Sulu Spiny Lobster
description: A native goby species inhabiting estuaries, brackish mangroves, and river mouths. Plays an important role in coastal food webs.
location: Coral reefs of the Sulu Sea near Zamboanga City.

name: Mindanao Seahorse
description: Endemic seahorse living in seagrass beds and mangroves; vulnerable due to habitat loss.
location: Seagrass meadows of Zamboanga del Sur and Pagadian.

name: Zamboanga Snapper
description: Local snapper variants dominating reef slopes; target of commercial fisheries.
location: Reef slopes off Zamboanga Peninsula facing Sulu Sea.

name: Flying Fish
description: Flying fish capable of gliding above water surface, commonly seen in pelagic zones.
location: Pelagic surface waters of Zamboanga del Norte and Basilan Strait.

name: Philippine Pygmy Seahorse
description: Small seahorse blending with soft corals; vulnerable to reef degradation.
location: Offshore reefs of Zamboanga del Sur

name: Tulingan
description: Migratory small tuna important to local fisheries.
location: Open waters of the Sulu Sea near Zamboanga.

name: Endemic Blue Dot Grouper
description: Colorful reef predator abundant in coral thickets; fishery target.
location: Sibuguey Bay coral reefs

name: Zamboanga Wrasse
description: Reef wrasses functioning as cleaners and algae grazers.
location: Fringing reefs of Zamboanga del Norte.

name: Mangrove Crab
description: Mud crab harvested from brackish mangroves; important coastal resource.
location: Mangrove swamps of Labangan and Dipolog estuaries.

name: Sea Grape
description: Edible green algae, harvested sustainably.
location: Seagrass beds in Zamboanga Sibugay.

name: Sulu Sea Anemonefish
description: Hardy clownfish living symbiotically with anemones.
location: Coral reefs and lagoons in Zamboanga Peninsula.

name: Spotted Cardinalfish
description: Nocturnal reef fish feeding on zooplankton.
location: Reef caves along Zamboanga’s southern coast.

name: Zamboanga Flasher Wrasse
description: Display fish with vibrant mating colors.
location: Outer reef slopes near Olutanga.

name: Reef Octopus
description: Intelligent cephalopod, frequent in reef flats.
location: Coral rubble flats near Zamboanga City.

name: Needlefish
description: Surface predator preying on small fish.
location: Coastal waters near Isabela City.

name: Giant Trevally
description: Powerful reef predator, prized in sport fishing.
location: Basilan Strait reefs.

name: Spotted Pufferfish
description: Inflates defensively; common in lagoons.
location: Lagoon systems of Zamboanga del Sur.

name: Mottled Sea Cucumber
description: Detritivore important for sand turnover.
location: Sandy reef bottoms in Zamboanga Peninsula.

name: Short-bodied Mackerel
description: Schooling commercial fish.
location: Fish landing ports of Zamboanga City.

name: Sulu Lionfish
description: Venomous spiny fish with striking fins, found in coral reefs.
location: Reefs near Isabela City and Zamboanga coast.

name: Chocolate Chip Sea Star
description: Large sea star with dark tips, feeding on organic matter.
location: Sandy shallows and seagrass beds of Sibuguey Bay.

name: Zamboanga Feather Star
description: Colorful crinoid filter feeder clinging to coral branches.
location: Coral slopes of Zamboanga del Sur.

name: Sea Goldie
description: Schooling reef fish with brilliant orange color
location: Drop-offs and reef crests in Zamboanga Peninsula.

name: Philippine Porcupinefish
description: Inflates and erects spines when threatened.
location: Reef flats and rubble near Olutanga

name: Orange-spotted Spinefoot
description: Herbivore often found in pairs, common in reef lagoons.
location: Coral gardens off Zamboanga City.

name: Blackspot Snapper
description: Small snapper with a black spot near tail; omnivorous.
location: Nearshore reefs of Zamboanga Sibugay.

name: Midas Blenny
description: Reef blenny known for its yellow body and mimic behavior.
location: Coral crevices in shallow reefs.

name: Tiger Cowrie
description: Glossy-shelled mollusk known for its spotted pattern.
location: Under rocks and coral in reef flats.

name: Indian Mackerel
description: Schooling pelagic fish caught in coastal fisheries.
location: Open waters near Zamboanga City.
